text.title: Chunking Code AST — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules: dispatch by language, AST primary path, dedup overlapping chunks, log on fallback, version on strategy change.

rule.statement: CodeChunker MUST dispatch on language: Python goes through `ast.parse` + node walk; JS/TS goes through tree-sitter (regex only as last-resort fallback); other languages go to the generic line splitter.
rule.rationale: A single regex path on a multi-language corpus mis-splits decorators, generics, and arrow functions. Language dispatch is the only path that scales.

rule.statement: Python files MUST be chunked via `ast.parse` walking FunctionDef, AsyncFunctionDef, ClassDef. Each chunk MUST carry name, type, docstring, start_line, end_line.

rationale.source: Python ast docs + code-search retrieval benchmarks

rule.rationale: Decorators, type annotations, and nested definitions are handled correctly only by the AST path. Regex splitters lose nested-class boundaries.

rule.statement: When the AST walk emits both a class chunk and its method chunks, the emitted list MUST be deduplicated by (name, start_line) before indexing, OR the class-level chunk MUST be excluded.
rule.rationale: Overlapping chunks inflate the index and skew k-NN scores toward classes with many methods. Dedup keeps ranking stable.

rule.statement: Any fallback from AST/tree-sitter to the generic line splitter MUST log a structured warning with file, language, reason so the caller can flag the file for re-ingest after the parse issue is fixed.
rule.rationale: Silent fallback ships chunks without name/type/docstring, breaking name-based retrieval. The warning is the only handle the caller has.

rule.statement: Changing the chunking strategy (e.g. AST then tree-sitter, or different dedup policy) MUST bump strategy_version and write to a versioned collection name. Mixed-strategy indices MUST NOT exist.
rule.rationale: Mixing strategies under one collection name causes hits from old chunks to surface alongside new ones, with incompatible metadata.
